summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by welcoming the congregation and celebrating recent events before delivering a powerful message titled 'Don't Go Back.' He began by posing a central question: why would anyone return to the situations, relationships, or addictions they had to pray themselves out of? Using the story of the Israelites in Exodus, Pastor Travis illustrated how God's people, after being miraculously freed from slavery in Egypt, later grumbled and wanted to return to the familiar bondage. He paralleled this to our personal 'Egypts'—past hurts, addictions, and destructive patterns from which God has delivered us. The core of the message was a call to move forward into God's promises and never retreat to what held us captive. Pastor Travis outlined four key understandings: first, that God has a divine, prosperous plan for each life, far greater than our earthly goals. Second, that God is just getting started with us, regardless of age, as illustrated by the story of 'Grandma Louise.' Third, that God sees potential and purpose in us that we often cannot see in ourselves, just as He did with Moses and Gideon. Finally, that walking into God's promised future requires leaving the past behind, straining and pressing forward. He emphasized that freedom, paid for by Jesus on the cross, requires effort and the support of a godly community. The sermon concluded with an invitation to surrender or rededicate one's life to Christ and a strong encouragement to get connected through small groups and weekly studies to stay strong and avoid returning to old patterns.
mainPoints: God has a divine and prosperous plan for your life that is far greater than any earthly goal, and you should never settle for less than His best.
God is just getting started with you and sees potential and purpose within you that you may not yet see in yourself.
To walk into God's promises and future, you must decisively leave your past—your personal 'Egypt'—behind and press forward with effort and godly support.

versesMentioned.text: Moreover, I have heard the groaning of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians are enslaving, and I have remembered my covenant. Therefore, say to the Israelites: ‘I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians. I will free you from being slaves to them, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with mighty acts of judgment. I will take you as my own people, and I will be your God. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God, who brought you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians.'

All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron, and the whole assembly said to them, 'If only we had died in Egypt! Or in this wilderness! Why is the LORD bringing us to this land only to let us fall by the sword? Our wives and children will be taken as plunder. Wouldn’t it be better for us to go back to Egypt?' And they said to each other, 'We should choose a leader and go back to Egypt.'
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.
'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the LORD, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.'

For you created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well. My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place, when I was woven together in the depths of the earth. Your eyes saw my unformed body; all the days ordained for me were written in your book before one of them came to be.
being confident of this, that he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.
Don’t let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith and in purity.
So now, go. I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring my people the Israelites out of Egypt.
When the angel of the LORD appeared to Gideon, he said, 'The LORD is with you, mighty warrior.'
Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the wasteland.

Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!

Every day they continued to meet together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts, praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.

Monday.scripture: Exodus 6:6-7 (NIV) - 'I will free you from being slaves... and I will redeem you... I will take you as my own people, and I will be your God.'
Monday.action: Identify one 'Egypt' from your past that you are tempted to return to. Write it down and pray for strength to leave it behind.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for setting me free. Help me to see and reject any thought of returning to old bondage. I choose your freedom today.

Tuesday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:11 (NIV) - 'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the LORD, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.'
Tuesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection, asking God to reveal one step you can take this week toward His purpose for your life.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I trust that your plans for me are good. Open my eyes to your purpose and give me the courage to pursue it, not my own.

Wednesday.scripture: Philippians 1:6 (NIV) - '...he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion...'
Wednesday.action: Connect with a fellow believer—call, text, or attend a small group. Share one way you've seen God working in your life recently.
Wednesday.prayer: God, thank you that you are still working in me. Help me to stay connected to your body, the church, for strength and encouragement.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 43:18-19 (NIV) - 'Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing!'
Thursday.action: Intentionally replace a negative thought about your past with a promise from God's Word. Write down the promise and carry it with you.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, help me to forget what lies behind. Give me faith to perceive the new thing you are doing in my life right now.

Friday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - '...Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on...'
Friday.action: Do one difficult but healthy thing you've been avoiding that will help you move forward (e.g., a difficult conversation, ending a bad habit, starting a new discipline).
Friday.prayer: Jesus, give me the strength to strain and press on. My freedom cost you everything; help me to value it enough to fight for it today.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 (NIV) - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!'
Saturday.action: Prepare for worship tomorrow by confessing any area where you've looked back this week. Receive God's forgiveness and reaffirm your identity in Christ.
Saturday.prayer: Thank you, Father, for making me new. I renounce my old ways and declare that I am your new creation, called to walk in freedom and purpose.
